fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: New York Speech-Language Pathologist License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a speech-language pathologist? Here's whether your license transfers to New York - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: speech-language pathologist license reciprocity new york, how to transfer speech-language pathologist license to new york, speech-language pathologist compact new york, new york speech-language pathologist endorsement application

board.name: State Board for Speech-Language Pathology and Audiology
board.parent_dept: New York State Education Department (NYSED), Office of the Professions
board.mailing_address: New York State Education Department, Office of the Professions, Speech-Language Pathologists and Audiologists Unit, 89 Washington Avenue, Albany, NY 12234-1000

license_types.title: Licensed Speech-Language Pathologist
license_types.description: The full credential to independently practice speech-language pathology in New York. Requires a master's degree, completion of 36 weeks supervised experience, and a passing Praxis score. Applicants must be at least 21 years old.

license_types.title: Limited License (SLP)
license_types.description: Issued to SLP graduates completing their supervised professional experience. Allows practice under supervision while fulfilling the 36-week experience requirement. Fee: $70.

steps.title: Meet Education Requirements
steps.description: Complete a Master's or Doctor's degree in Speech-Language Pathology from a program registered by the NYSED or determined by the Department to be equivalent. This education must include at least 400 clock hours of supervised clinical practicum.

steps.title: Pass Praxis Examination
steps.description: Achieve a passing score on the Praxis Subject Assessment in Speech-Language Pathology (Code 5031) administered by ETS. You must request ETS to send your official score report directly to the NYSED Office of the Professions.

steps.title: Complete Supervised Experience
steps.description: Complete a supervised clinical experience, often referred to as a Clinical Fellowship Year (CFY), consisting of 1200 hours over a minimum of 36 weeks. This experience must be attested to on Form 4B by your supervisor and submitted directly to NYSED.

steps.title: Submit Application and Fee
steps.description: Download and complete the 'Application for Licensure' (Form 1) from the NYSED Office of the Professions website. Include the required application fee of $294. Mail the complete form and fee to the Office of the Professions.

steps.title: Submit Supporting Documentation
steps.description: Arrange for your college or university to send official transcripts, verifying your degree and supervised practicum hours, directly to NYSED using 'Certification of Professional Education' (Form 2). Ensure your supervisor submits 'Verification of Supervised Experience' (Form 4B) directly to NYSED.

steps.title: Await License Issuance
steps.description: The NYSED Office of the Professions will review your complete application and all supporting documents. Allow adequate time for processing; you will be notified of your license status upon completion of the review.

documents.detail: Certification of Professional Education from a CAA-accredited program. May incur an additional institutional fee.
Score of 162 or higher on test 5331. Sent directly to NYSED using agency code R7747.
Verification of 36 weeks of supervised professional experience signed by supervisor.
Completion certificates for mandated New York coursework in child abuse identification and harassment prevention.

telepractice_text: New York permits telepractice for licensed SLPs. The practitioner must hold a valid New York SLP license to provide services to clients located in New York State. New York does not currently participate in the ASLP Interstate Compact, so out-of-state SLPs must obtain New York licensure before providing telepractice services to New York residents.
provisional_text: New York issues a Limited License for SLP graduates completing their supervised professional experience. The Limited License costs $70 and allows practice under supervision while fulfilling the 36-week experience requirement. The supervising SLP must hold a current New York license.
reciprocity_text: New York does not have formal reciprocity agreements or participate in the ASLP Interstate Compact. Out-of-state SLPs must apply for full New York licensure and meet all state-specific requirements. ASHA CCC-SLP holders must still submit all required forms and documentation to NYSED.

faqs.question: How much does a New York SLP license cost?
faqs.answer: The total initial licensure and registration fee is $294, submitted with Form 1 to NYSED. A Limited License for supervised practice costs an additional $70. Triennial renewal is $155.

faqs.question: Do I need a TSSLD to work in New York schools?
faqs.answer: Yes. To work as an SLP in a New York school setting, you need the Teacher of Students with Speech and Language Disability (TSSLD) credential in addition to your state SLP license. The TSSLD is issued by NYSED and has separate application requirements.

faqs.question: How long does the New York SLP application take?
faqs.answer: Processing time is approximately six to eight weeks once all documents are received by NYSED. Incomplete applications will cause significant delays. Submit all forms and fees together to avoid processing issues.

faqs.question: Does New York have reciprocity for speech-language pathologists?
faqs.answer: No interstate compact currently covers speech-language pathologist practice in New York. To work in New York, apply for licensure by endorsement through the state board.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my speech-language pathologist license to New York?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 6 to 8 weeks.

faqs.question: Can I start working in New York while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

reciprocity.notes: New York does not have formal reciprocity agreements or participate in the ASLP Interstate Compact. Out-of-state SLPs must apply for full New York licensure and meet all state-specific requirements. ASHA CCC-SLP holders must still submit all required forms and documentation to NYSED.

hero.title: Become a Speech-Language Pathologist in New York
hero.dek: To apply for a Speech-Language Pathologist license in New York, download and complete the required forms from the New York State Education Department (NYSED) Office of the Professions website and mail them in.

official_links.portal_name: NYSED Office of the Professions Forms
official_links.registration_steps: To start your application for a Speech-Language Pathologist license in New York, you must download and complete the required forms (such as Form 1 for initial application) from the NYSED Office of the Professions website. Mail the completed forms, along with all supporting documentation and the application fee, to the Office of the Professions. After licensure, you can create an account on NYSED Online Services for managing your license, including renewals.
